dominantJob.reasoning: Because there are multiple primary-styled orange buttons scattered across both the feed list and the detail view, which violates the single primary action principle — compared to Linear's Inbox which centralizes actions in the detail pane — this scores 4/10.
violations: Orange 'Open in Chat' button on 'Report changed' card
Orange 'Open report' button on 'Reply draft ready' card
Orange 'Open in Chat' button on 'Reminder due' card
Three interactive pills in the top header box
Connected Channels section competing for attention

visibleReasoning.reasoning: Because the UI explicitly breaks down the AI's reasoning process into three numbered steps in the right column and provides context for each nudge, which strongly aligns with visible reasoning — compared to Perplexity's inline citations — this scores 8/10.
trustSignals: Contextual explanations in nudge cards ('A saved report picked up a new source')
Explicit mention of 'saved memory' connecting past actions to current nudges

speedBehavior.reasoning: Because the layout relies on stable, bordered containers that appear to have fixed dimensions, which prevents layout shifts — compared to Vercel's strict skeleton loading patterns — this scores 7/10.
latencyIssues: Lack of visible progressive disclosure for the feed (appears static)
Potential for layout shift if the right column content changes dynamically based on left column selection

qualityDiscipline.reasoning: Because there is a proliferation of pill styles and button sizes that dilute the design system, which violates strict token consistency — compared to Linear's rigorous component reuse — this scores 6/10.
papercuts: Inconsistent button sizes (small vs large 'Open in Chat')
Multiple pill styles (outline vs filled, different text colors)
Alignment of section headers vs page header padding

contextCompounding.reasoning: Because the copy heavily references saved memory, previous reports, and connected channels, which strongly indicates the system learns and compounds context — compared to Notion AI's document-aware generation — this scores 9/10.
compoundingSignals: 'Connected Channels' section indicating external data integration

chromeCollapse.reasoning: Because nearly every distinct piece of content is wrapped in a 1px border with rounded corners, which violates the principle of minimizing chrome — compared to ChatGPT's borderless, whitespace-driven hierarchy — this scores 3/10.
excessChrome: 3 step box borders in right column

components.name: Top Navigation
components.reasoning: Standard and necessary for app navigation across the 5 surfaces.

components.name: Main Header Box
components.beforeInteraction: Static text and informational pills inside a bordered container
components.reasoning: The large border is unnecessary; typography alone can establish this hierarchy and context.

components.name: Feed Cards (Left Column)
components.beforeInteraction: List of bordered items with inline primary buttons
components.reasoning: Remove the inline primary buttons to make the card itself the clickable element that updates the detail view, reducing competing CTAs.

components.name: Detail View (Right Column)
components.beforeInteraction: Shows details and reasoning of selected nudge
components.afterInteraction: Navigates to Chat surface with context
components.reasoning: Excellent breakdown of reasoning and clear primary CTA for the selected item.

components.name: Connected Channels Box
components.reasoning: Belongs in settings or a dedicated integrations page, not competing with the primary nudge workflow on this screen.

competitorComparison: Compared to Linear's Inbox, NodeBench's Nudges surface is too heavily boxed and scatters primary actions across the list view rather than centralizing them. However, NodeBench excels at visible reasoning, explicitly breaking down why a nudge was generated in a way that builds trust, similar to Perplexity's citation model.
topIssues: Excessive use of bordered containers (chrome) creates visual clutter and a boxed-in feel.
Multiple primary-styled 'Open in Chat' buttons in the feed compete for attention with the main detail CTA.
'Connected Channels' section distracts from the main nudge workflow and belongs in settings.
Inconsistent pill and button styles dilute the design system and create visual noise.
The main header is trapped in a box, reducing the open feel of the layout.
recommendations: Remove borders from the feed cards and use whitespace or subtle background fills for separation.
Remove the inline orange buttons from the left feed cards; make the entire card clickable to update the right pane.
Remove the main header's bounding box to let the typography establish hierarchy.
Move 'Connected Channels' to a settings page or a dedicated onboarding flow.
Standardize pill styles (e.g., 'EXAMPLE', 'REPORT CHANGED') to a single visual token.
